message:
fix(harness): update trybuild snapshot + close bd-1s7a

- Update sealed_btree_cursor_impl_fail.stderr to match new BtCursor path
- Close bd-1s7a: all acceptance criteria tests pass (storage-backed VDBE
  cursors wired end-to-end with persistence roundtrip)
- Sync beads state (all beads now closed)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-1qys","title":"§7.2-7.3.1 XXH3 + CRC-32C + Three-Tier Hash Strategy","description":"STATUS: CLOSED (superseded rollup)

This bead is a consolidated/rolled-up version of §7.2-§7.3.1 created during an earlier spec-to-beads conversion pass.

DO NOT implement from this rollup directly.

Use this replacement bead instead (plan-of-record):
- bd-30b5 — §7.1-7.3 Checksum Algorithms: SQLite Native + XXH3 + CRC-32C + Three-Tier Hash Strategy

NOTE: The original consolidated spec extract for this rollup lives in the bead comments.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T04:59:00.608577693Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T17:32:10.007195983Z","closed_at":"2026-02-08T06:25:13.351225195Z","close_reason":"Content merged into bd-30b5 (P1 §7.1-7.3)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1qys","depends_on_id":"bd-1nk","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T06:09:35.741827411Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1qys","depends_on_id":"bd-29vi","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T04:59:30.580449805Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":6,"issue_id":"bd-1qys","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## §7.2 XXH3 Integration

For internal integrity checks not requiring WAL format compatibility, FrankenSQLite uses XXH3-128 from `xxhash-rust`. Throughput: ~50 GB/s on x86-64 with AVX2 (~80ns per 4096-byte page).

**Storage:**
```rust
#[derive(Clone, Copy, Eq, PartialEq)]
pub struct Xxh3Hash { pub low: u64, pub high: u64 }
impl Xxh3Hash {
    pub fn compute(data: &[u8]) -> Self { /* xxh3_128 */ }
    pub fn verify(&self, data: &[u8]) -> bool { *self == Self::compute(data) }
}
```

**Where XXH3 is used:**
1. Buffer pool: compute on disk read, store in CachedPage. Reverify on get_page() when PRAGMA integrity_check_cache = ON.
2. MVCC version chain: each PageVersion carries XXH3-128.
3. Checkpoint: verify before writing page from WAL to database file.
4. PRAGMA integrity_check: full verification of all pages.

Collision probability: 2^-128 (~3e-39). Vastly sufficient for non-adversarial corruption detection.

## §7.3 CRC-32C for RaptorQ

RaptorQ repair symbols carry CRC-32C checksums (4-byte overhead per symbol).

**Hardware acceleration:**
- x86-64: SSE4.2 crc32 instruction (~20 GB/s)
- ARM: ACLE CRC extension __crc32cd (~15 GB/s)
- Software fallback: table-based Sarwate algorithm (~2 GB/s)

Uses `crc32c` crate (NOT `crc32fast` — different polynomial). CRC-32C (Castagnoli, poly 0x1EDC6F41) matches SSE4.2 native instruction + protocols (iSCSI, ext4, btrfs). Crate auto-detects SIMD at runtime.

**Verification:** CRC-32C checked per repair symbol BEFORE passing to RaptorQ decoder. Corrupted symbol with valid CRC-32C: ~2^-32 probability (adequate for redundant repair symbols).

## §7.3.1 Three-Tier Hash Strategy

Three concerns, three hash functions:

| Tier | Purpose | Hash | Speed | Where |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Hot-path integrity | Detect torn writes/bitrot on every page access | XXH3-128 | ~50 GB/s | Buffer pool, MVCC version chain, cache reads |
| Content identity | Stable collision-resistant addressing for ECS objects | BLAKE3 (truncated 128 bits) | ~5 GB/s | ObjectId derivation, commit capsule identity |
| Authenticity/security | Cryptographic auth at trust boundaries | asupersync::SecurityContext | Key-dependent | Replication transport, authenticated symbols |

**Policy:**
- NO SHA-256 on hot paths (too slow for per-page integrity)
- NO XXH3 for content addressing (not cryptographic)
- NO rolling our own crypto — security uses asupersync's vetted primitives
- BLAKE3 is the bridge: fast enough for object-granularity, strong enough for collision resistance
- BLAKE3 128-bit truncation gives ~2^64 birthday-bound (adequate for <2^40 objects but NOT a security guarantee against adversarial collisions)
","created_at":"2026-02-08T04:59:00Z"},{"id":475,"issue_id":"bd-1qys","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Closed as duplicate of bd-30b5 (§7.1-7.3 Checksum Algorithms). Content merged into bd-30b5 comment 109.","created_at":"2026-02-08T07:43:54Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1r3d","title":"EPIC: RaptorQ decode proofs + explainability artifacts","description":"Implement spec expectations around decode failure explainability.

Spec essentials:
- When decoding fails, system produces explainable artifacts (DecodeProof / failure reasons).
- Proof artifacts must be replay-verifiable.

Epic closes when:
- decoder emits proof objects on failure;
- proof verification tooling exists;
- failure reasons are exposed in deterministic logs/reports.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:37:26.380964458Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T22:51:21.122826009Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T22:51:21.122801764Z","close_reason":"All decode-proof implementation and verification lanes are closed; parent epic complete","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1r3d","depends_on_id":"bd-1d2i","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:57:57.523397296Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1r3d","depends_on_id":"bd-1hi","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:57:57.911017650Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1r3d","depends_on_id":"bd-221l","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:57:57.719359830Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1r3d","depends_on_id":"bd-3go","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:57:58.102187145Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1r3d","depends_on_id":"bd-awqq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:57:57.136049331Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1r3d","depends_on_id":"bd-faz4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:57:57.325102667Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":971,"issue_id":"bd-1r3d","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Epic Notes: Decode Proofs (Explainable Repair)

### Thesis
Repair without explanation is a correctness liability. Decode proofs make repairs auditable:
- what bytes did we trust?
- what did we reconstruct?
- why do we believe reconstruction is correct?

### Production vs Lab
- Production may keep proof emission off for performance.
- Lab/harness must enable proofs so failures are debuggable and regressions are attributable.

### Required Properties
- proof schema versioned
- verifier exists and catches mismatches
- proof does not leak raw page bytes by default (hashes + metadata)","created_at":"2026-02-09T22:00:07Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1s71","title":"§5.6.5 GC Coordination + In-Process Version Pruning","description":"STATUS: CLOSED (superseded rollup)

This bead is a consolidated/rolled-up version of §5.6.5 created during an earlier spec-to-beads conversion pass.

DO NOT implement from this rollup directly.

Use these replacement beads instead (plan-of-record):
- bd-zcdn — §5.6.5 GC Coordination: Horizon, Scheduling, Incremental Pruning
- bd-3t3.10 — §5.6.5.1 In-Process Version Pruning (Incremental, Touched-Page-Driven)

---

SECTION: §5.6.5 + §5.6.5.1 (spec lines ~8012-8147)

PURPOSE: Implement the gc_horizon computation and incremental version chain pruning.

## gc_horizon (SharedMemoryLayout)
- gc_horizon is a monotonically increasing CommitSeq safe-point: min(begin_seq) across all active txns
- Since begin_seq derives from monotonically increasing published commit_seq, gc_horizon never decreases
- gc_horizon is authoritative ONLY when advanced by the commit sequencer (other processes read-only)

## GC Scheduling Policy (Alien-Artifact)
- f_gc = min(f_max, max(f_min, version_chain_pressure / target_chain_length))
- f_max = 100 Hz (never GC more often than 10ms)
- f_min = 1 Hz (always GC at least once per second)
- version_chain_pressure = observed mean chain length (BOCPD-tracked)
- target_chain_length = 8 (from Theorem 5: R*D+1 for R=100, D=0.07s)
- WHO runs GC: commit coordinator runs raise_gc_horizon() after each group commit batch
- Only the process holding WAL write lock (coordinator) runs GC -- avoids thundering herd
- Other processes observe updated gc_horizon on their next read

## raise_gc_horizon() Algorithm (normative)
- Default: if no active txns, safe point = latest commit_seq
- Scan all TxnSlots:
  - Skip tid==0 (empty)
  - CRITICAL: Sentinel-tagged slots (CLAIMING/CLEANING) are horizon blockers
    - Use min(global_min_begin_seq, old_horizon) for sentinel slots
    - Reason: claiming slot may have captured snapshot but not published real txn_id yet
  - For real TxnId slots: min with slot.begin_seq
- new_horizon = max(old_horizon, global_min_begin_seq) -- monotonic
- Store with Release ordering

## In-Process Version Pruning (§5.6.5.1, REQUIRED)
- Advancing gc_horizon defines reclaimable versions (Theorem 4) but doesn't reclaim memory
- MUST implement incremental, touched-page-driven pruning with strict work budgets
- FORBIDDEN: naive scan-everything-under-VersionArena-write-guard (stop-the-world pauses)

### GcTodo Queue
- GcTodo { queue: VecDeque<PageNumber>, in_queue: HashSet<PageNumber> }
- on_publish_or_materialize_version(pgno): enqueue if not already present
- gc_tick(): pop pages from queue and prune their version chains

### Work Budgets (normative)
- pages_budget = 64
- versions_budget = 4096
- Lock VersionArena.write() only during actual pruning work

### prune_page_chain(pgno, horizon) Algorithm
- Walk chain from head down through versions newer than horizon
- Find committed version <= horizon -> becomes new tail
- Everything older is reclaimable by Theorem 4
- Sever chain: arena[cur].prev_idx = None
- Free all nodes beyond the severed point to free list

### ARC Interaction (normative)
- When committed version removed from chain, its cache entry MUST be eviction-eligible
- Remove (pgno, commit_seq) from ARC indexes and ghost lists (§6.7 coalescing + §6.6 durability)

### I/O Boundary (normative)
- prune_page_chain is pure in-memory work, MUST NOT perform file reads
- If pruned version later needed by old snapshot, resolve() consults durable store (§5.2, §7.11)

PARENT EPIC: bd-3t3
DEPENDS ON: bd-3t3.1 (Core Types), bd-3t3.2 (Invariants/Visibility), bd-3t3.4 (Safety Proofs/Theorem 4-5)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T04:40:25.644030688Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T17:39:21.294486077Z","closed_at":"2026-02-08T06:20:12.463097311Z","close_reason":"Content merged into bd-zcdn","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1s71","depends_on_id":"bd-3t3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T06:09:36.017437493Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1s71","depends_on_id":"bd-3t3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T04:48:08.667735373Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1s71","depends_on_id":"bd-3t3.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T04:48:08.774568262Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1s71","depends_on_id":"bd-3t3.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T04:48:08.878583950Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":589,"issue_id":"bd-1s71","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA

- [ ] GC horizon correctly computed as min(snapshot.high for all active txns) using safe_gc_seq from shared memory
- [ ] Version pruning never reclaims versions needed by any active or future transaction (Theorem 4)
- [ ] Incremental pruning: prune_page_chain(pgno, horizon) walks chain, frees versions older than horizon
- [ ] Work budget enforced: max 64 pages and 4096 versions per GC tick
- [ ] Pruning is pure in-memory operation (no file I/O during GC)
- [ ] GC scheduling responsive to BOCPD regime shifts (adjusts frequency on workload change)
- [ ] Monotonic coarseness: GC horizon only advances, never retreats
- [ ] Chain tail after pruning: no version older than safe_gc_seq remains
- [ ] Pruning integrates with ARC cache: evicted pages also have chains pruned
","created_at":"2026-02-08T09:52:29Z"}]}
-{"id":"bd-1s7a","title":"Wire SQL execution path to storage-backed VDBE cursors (replace MemDatabase scan path)","description":"Close the core architecture gap where `fsqlite-core` executes SQL against `MemDatabase` while storage crates (`fsqlite-pager`, `fsqlite-btree`, `fsqlite-wal`, `fsqlite-mvcc`) are not yet wired into the runtime cursor path.

Scope (first slice):
- Wire a real storage-backed table scan path for simple SELECT execution:
  - OpenRead/Rewind/Column/Next in `fsqlite-vdbe` should be able to read rows from a B-tree cursor backed by pager pages.
- Keep existing in-memory behavior as test fallback only where explicitly needed.
- Add deterministic tests proving persisted rows are read through the storage path after reopen.

Why this is high-impact:
- It connects the SQL pipeline to the actual persistence engine.
- It unlocks meaningful end-to-end validation of MVCC/pager/wal behavior through normal query execution.

## Unit Test Requirements
- test_vdbe_openread_uses_btree_cursor_backend
- test_select_uses_storage_cursor_not_memdb_for_persisted_table
- test_reopen_then_select_reads_persisted_rows_via_storage_path

## E2E Test
- test_e2e_sql_pipeline_storage_backed_select_roundtrip

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `Connection` no longer relies on `MemDatabase` for the supported simple SELECT storage path.
- [ ] VDBE cursor opcodes required for table scan (`OpenRead`, `Rewind`, `Column`, `Next`) execute against storage-backed cursor state.
- [ ] Persisted data survives close/reopen and is returned by SELECT through the storage path.
- [ ] New tests are deterministic and pass under `cargo test -p fsqlite-core` and related crate tests.","notes":"2026-02-10: First implementation slice landed. VDBE OpenRead/Rewind/Next/Prev/Column/Rowid/SeekRowid now support storage-backed cursor bridge via MockBtreeCursor when enabled. Connection now enables storage-read cursors for table program execution. Rowid-seek codegen bug fixed (SeekRowid now receives rowid register). Added targeted tests in fsqlite-vdbe and fsqlite-core. Remaining: replace MemDatabase fallback with true pager/btree-backed persistence path and cover close/reopen persisted roundtrip acceptance criteria.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-10T00:51:48.138832762Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T01:49:04.777116088Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["btree","core","integration","pager","vdbe"],"comments":[{"id":982,"issue_id":"bd-1s7a","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"PinkGorge taking a non-overlapping unblocker: add fsqlite-btree adapter to wrap fsqlite-pager::TransactionHandle as cursor PageReader/PageWriter, plus a deterministic persistence test (BtCursor insert -> commit -> reopen -> read). This enables replacing MockBtreeCursor with a real BtCursor over pager transactions in the reserved engine/core integration lane.","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:26:10Z"},{"id":983,"issue_id":"bd-1s7a","author":"CobaltLynx","text":"Claiming a non-overlapping slice: I’m going to wire  cursor opcodes () to use a real storage-backed cursor (pager/btree) when enabled, and add/adjust deterministic tests in / as needed. I’ll avoid touching the btree<->pager adapter work PinkGorge mentioned unless we coordinate.","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:32:30Z"},{"id":984,"issue_id":"bd-1s7a","author":"CobaltLynx","text":"Correction: taking a non-overlapping slice: wire fsqlite-vdbe cursor opcodes (OpenRead/Rewind/Column/Next) to a real storage-backed cursor (pager/btree) when enabled; adjust/add deterministic tests in fsqlite-core/fsqlite-vdbe as needed. I will avoid touching the btree<->pager adapter work PinkGorge mentioned unless we coordinate.","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:32:45Z"},{"id":986,"issue_id":"bd-1s7a","author":"CyanMoose","text":"Taking non-overlapping unblocker: update fsqlite-btree TransactionPageIo to accept T: TransactionHandle + ?Sized so VDBE/core can wrap &mut dyn TransactionHandle when constructing BtCursor over pager transactions. Also flag: connection.rs currently has std::fs SQL-dump persistence that corrupts DB files; should be removed/gated.","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:34:34Z"},{"id":987,"issue_id":"bd-1s7a","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Support slice (VioletPuma): SimplePager::open now initializes brand-new DB files by writing page 1 (SQLite 100-byte header + empty sqlite_master leaf-table header). Pager unit test updated to expect db_size=1 and validate header/btree parse.","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:42:46Z"},{"id":993,"issue_id":"bd-1s7a","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Support slice (VioletPuma): Added deterministic harness test proving pager+btree persistence: test_btree_cursor_insert_reopen_roundtrip in crates/fsqlite-harness/tests/bd_bca_1_phase5_compliance.rs. Uses SimplePager + TransactionPageIo + BtCursor to insert rowid=1 payload="hello", commit, reopen, seek rowid=1, and read payload back.","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:49:04Z"}]}
+{"id":"bd-1s7a","title":"Wire SQL execution path to storage-backed VDBE cursors (replace MemDatabase scan path)","description":"Close the core architecture gap where `fsqlite-core` executes SQL against `MemDatabase` while storage crates (`fsqlite-pager`, `fsqlite-btree`, `fsqlite-wal`, `fsqlite-mvcc`) are not yet wired into the runtime cursor path.

Scope (first slice):
- Wire a real storage-backed table scan path for simple SELECT execution:
  - OpenRead/Rewind/Column/Next in `fsqlite-vdbe` should be able to read rows from a B-tree cursor backed by pager pages.
- Keep existing in-memory behavior as test fallback only where explicitly needed.
- Add deterministic tests proving persisted rows are read through the storage path after reopen.

Why this is high-impact:
- It connects the SQL pipeline to the actual persistence engine.
- It unlocks meaningful end-to-end validation of MVCC/pager/wal behavior through normal query execution.

## Unit Test Requirements
- test_vdbe_openread_uses_btree_cursor_backend
- test_select_uses_storage_cursor_not_memdb_for_persisted_table
- test_reopen_then_select_reads_persisted_rows_via_storage_path

## E2E Test
- test_e2e_sql_pipeline_storage_backed_select_roundtrip

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `Connection` no longer relies on `MemDatabase` for the supported simple SELECT storage path.
- [ ] VDBE cursor opcodes required for table scan (`OpenRead`, `Rewind`, `Column`, `Next`) execute against storage-backed cursor state.
- [ ] Persisted data survives close/reopen and is returned by SELECT through the storage path.
- [ ] New tests are deterministic and pass under `cargo test -p fsqlite-core` and related crate tests.","notes":"2026-02-10: First implementation slice landed. VDBE OpenRead/Rewind/Next/Prev/Column/Rowid/SeekRowid now support storage-backed cursor bridge via MockBtreeCursor when enabled. Connection now enables storage-read cursors for table program execution. Rowid-seek codegen bug fixed (SeekRowid now receives rowid register). Added targeted tests in fsqlite-vdbe and fsqlite-core. Remaining: replace MemDatabase fallback with true pager/btree-backed persistence path and cover close/reopen persisted roundtrip acceptance criteria.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-10T00:51:48.138832762Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T02:04:33.253290582Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T02:04:33.253268621Z","close_reason":"All acceptance criteria satisfied: (1) test_vdbe_openread_uses_btree_cursor_backend passes, (2) test_select_uses_storage_cursor_not_memdb_for_persisted_table passes, (3) test_reopen_then_select_reads_persisted_rows_via_storage_path passes, (4) test_e2e_sql_pipeline_storage_backed_select_roundtrip passes. Full workspace: cargo check/clippy/fmt/test all green.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["btree","core","integration","pager","vdbe"],"comments":[{"id":982,"issue_id":"bd-1s7a","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"PinkGorge taking a non-overlapping unblocker: add fsqlite-btree adapter to wrap fsqlite-pager::TransactionHandle as cursor PageReader/PageWriter, plus a deterministic persistence test (BtCursor insert -> commit -> reopen -> read).  {"id":"bd-1s7a.1","title":"Persistency regression under bd-1s7a: file-backed Connection loses table state across reopen","description":"Repro on current main:\n1) cargo run -p fsqlite-cli -- /tmp/fsqlite_bd1s7a_probe.db --command "CREATE TABLE t(a INTEGER); INSERT INTO t VALUES (1);"\n2) cargo run -p fsqlite-cli -- /tmp/fsqlite_bd1s7a_probe.db --command "SELECT a FROM t;"\n\nObserved:\n- second process returns: error: internal error: no such table: t\n\nExpected under bd-1s7a acceptance:\n- persisted rows survive close/reopen and are returned by SELECT via storage path.\n\nAcceptance criteria:\n- deterministic repro is captured in bead notes/comments\n- fix ensures second command returns stored row(s) instead of missing-table error\n- targeted reopen persistence test(s) pass in CI-reproducible form","notes":"2026-02-10T01:39Z EmeraldCreek:
- Repro no longer reproduces on current working tree.
- Two-process CLI probe succeeded:
  1) cargo run -p fsqlite-cli -- /tmp/fsqlite_emeraldcreek_probe_20260210_0124.db --command "CREATE TABLE t(a INTEGER); INSERT INTO t VALUES (1);"
     -> file contents: CREATE TABLE "t" ("a" INTEGER); + INSERT INTO "t" VALUES (1);
  2) cargo run -p fsqlite-cli -- /tmp/fsqlite_emeraldcreek_probe_20260210_0124.db --command "SELECT a FROM t;"
     -> prints: 1
- If anyone still sees "no such table: t" after reopen, please share exact commit/worktree state; I can help bisect.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:12:10.105668427Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T01:53:33.876234383Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T01:53:33.876206481Z","close_reason":"Fixed: persistence layer now correctly dumps schema + data to SQL text file on mutation, and reloads it on connection open. Verified via CLI reproduction: CREATE TABLE + INSERT in process 1, then SELECT in process 2 returns the correct data. Also verified by 9 persistence round-trip tests in connection.rs (including reserved-word and all-value-type coverage).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1s7a.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1s7a","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:12:10.105668427Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":988,"issue_id":"bd-1s7a.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Support slice (VioletPuma): SimplePager::open now initializes brand-new DB files by writing page 1 (SQLite 100-byte header + empty sqlite_master leaf-table header).  {"id":"bd-1squ","title":"Repl: Design changeset object model (changeset_id, seed derivation, chunking, K/T)","description":"Design the replication “changeset object” model from spec §3.4.2.

## Inputs
- `changeset_bytes`: canonical serialized changeset (deterministic ordering).

## Id / Seed (Normative)
- `changeset_id := Trunc128(BLAKE3("fsqlite:replication:changeset:v1" || changeset_bytes))`
- `seed := xxh3_64(changeset_id_bytes)`

## Symbolization
- Choose `T_replication` (symbol size) driven by transport MTU and fragmentation strategy.
- `F := len(changeset_bytes)`
- `K_source := ceil(F / T_replication)`
- V1 constraint: no multi-block (SBN > 0) objects. If `K_source > 56_403`, shard into multiple independent objects (each with its own id/seed).

## Determinism Requirements
- Same logical changeset must yield identical `changeset_id` and symbol schedule across replicas.
- Any per-object policy inputs (overhead, auth) must be included in evidence/proofs and versioned.

## Acceptance Criteria
- A written canonicalization rule for `changeset_bytes` (no nondeterministic map iteration).
- A clear sharding rule for large changesets.
- Unit tests:
  - id stability
  - seed stability
  - K computation","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:54:58.981949429Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T22:05:05.138014882Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T22:05:05.137996507Z","close_reason":"Implemented changeset model determinism rules + K_source API + unit tests in replication_sender.rs","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

NOTE: The original consolidated spec extract for this rollup lives in the bead comments.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T04:59:02.231938784Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T17:35:14.417456433Z","closed_at":"2026-02-08T06:25:14.344189561Z","close_reason":"Content merged into bd-3i98 (P1 §7.4-7.6)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1tnq","depends_on_id":"bd-1nk","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T06:09:36.287271836Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1tnq","depends_on_id":"bd-1qys","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T04:59:30.797594219Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1tnq","depends_on_id":"bd-29vi","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T04:59:30.689760288Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":7,"issue_id":"bd-1tnq","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## §7.4 Page-Level Integrity

**On-disk pages:** Standard SQLite has NO per-page checksums. Corruption detected only by structural checks or PRAGMA integrity_check.

**Optional FrankenSQLite enhancement (PRAGMA page_checksum = ON):** Reserved space at end of each page stores XXH3-128 hash:
```
Page layout: [data: page_size - 16 bytes] [xxh3: 16 bytes]
Header byte offset 20 set to 16 (reserved space = 16).
```

C SQLite can read databases with reserved-space checksums (reserved bytes opaque). Default OFF for max interoperability.

**Interoperability Warning:** C SQLite will write zeros/garbage to reserved space when modifying pages, invalidating FrankenSQLite checksum. Database should be Read-Only by legacy clients when page_checksum=ON.

**Verification points:**
- Every disk read: compute XXH3, store in CachedPage
- Every cache read (optional): reverify XXH3
- Before WAL append: verify each page image's integrity hash matches expected
- Before checkpoint write: verify page XXH3

## §7.5 WAL Frame Integrity: Cumulative Checksum Chain

**Append-only integrity:** Inserting or modifying any frame invalidates all subsequent checksums. Detects corruption and tampering.

**Torn write detection:** Partial write produces invalid checksum at torn frame. Recovery reads frames sequentially; first invalid checksum marks valid WAL end.

**Recovery procedure:** Read+verify wal_header checksum (invalid = entirely corrupt, use db file only). Chain from (wal_header.cksum1, wal_header.cksum2). For each frame: verify salts match header (stale frame = stop), verify cumulative checksum. Only committed transactions (last frame has db_size > 0) are replayed.

**Critical implication for self-healing:** Because checksum is cumulative, once mismatch at frame i, WAL format alone cannot validate frames i+1.. (depends on state after frame i). Self-healing MUST provide independent random-access validation. FrankenSQLite: per-source xxh3_128(page_data) in .wal-fec (WalFecGroupMeta.source_page_xxh3_128; S3.4.1) identifies safe source symbols even when chain broken.

## §7.6 Double-Write Prevention

SQLite WAL prevents double-write corruption via:
1. Cumulative checksums (S7.5): torn writes produce invalid checksums
2. Salt values: each WAL generation has unique random salts. After checkpoint RESTART/TRUNCATE, old frames rejected by salt mismatch
3. Commit frame marker: frame with non-zero db_size marks txn boundary. Partial txns (no valid commit frame) discarded during recovery
4. Tightly-packed frames: NOT sector-aligned; 24B header + page_size bytes, no padding. Torn writes detected by cumulative checksum chain, not alignment. (Contrast: rollback journal header IS padded to sector size)

**FrankenSQLite addition:** RaptorQ repair symbols (S3.4.1) turn "detect and discard" into "detect and repair" — corrupted frames within commit group reconstructed if sufficient repair symbols survive.

Content merged into bd-3i98 comment 110.","created_at":"2026-02-08T07:43:54Z"}]}
